medications: Hydroxyzine 25 mg as needed for acute anxiety
Melatonin 3 mg at bedtime as needed
health.mental_health: In recovery from an eating disorder and managing anxiety that has flared since the kids left home; he reports intrusive worries, occasional panic symptoms, and a sense of emptiness/identity loss when the house is quiet. He’s actively in therapy and uses structured meal plans to avoid relapse triggers.
recent_visits: Weekly outpatient therapy session (ED/anxiety-focused) at a Richmond counseling practice
Monthly telehealth check-in with psychiatric NP for SSRI management
Primary care visit for sleep issues and reflux symptoms (last month)
Dietitian follow-up for recovery meal planning (two weeks ago)

finance.debt: Car loan (~$11k remaining) and credit card debt (~$3.2k) that grew during the transition after the kids moved out; he also sends small monthly support to one child in college, which strains his budget.
recent_transactions: Grocery delivery focused on planned meals ($86)
Monthly car payment to Navy Federal Credit Union ($312)
Transfer to child’s checking account for rent/books ($150)
Payment plan installment for dental work ($45)

relationships: Two children recently moved out (one in college out of state, one living with friends in Northern Virginia); he struggles with boundaries and frequent checking-in
Ex-partner and co-parent, Mara (communication is cordial but tense around parenting expectations)
Older sister, Priya (main emotional support; encourages therapy and socializing)
A small work friend circle (mostly pharmacy staff); he avoids discussing home life in detail
social.social_media_activity: Mostly passive scrolling on Instagram and Reddit; occasionally posts photos of hikes along the James River and meal-prep attempts. He has muted several family accounts because they trigger comparisons about parenting and “moving on.”
private_conversations: Discussing relapse fears and body-image thoughts with his therapist and sister
Negotiating co-parenting logistics and financial support with his ex-partner
Confiding to a coworker that he feels ashamed being an empty nester at 25 and worries people will judge him

schedule.work_hours: Rotating retail pharmacy schedule, typically 9:00am–5:30pm Mon–Fri with one closing shift (12:00pm–8:00pm) every other week and one Saturday shift (9:00am–2:00pm) about twice a month
regular_appointments: Medication management: first Friday of the month at 8:00am (telehealth)
upcoming_events: Weekend trip to visit child at college (planned for next month; hotel booked)
Dentist follow-up for filling replacement (next Tuesday at 3:30pm)
Work inventory count night (scheduled in two weeks)
schedule.daily_routine: Up around 6:45am, quick breakfast aligned with a meal plan, commute to the pharmacy, tries to take breaks on schedule to avoid skipping meals, evening walk near Libby Hill or along the Canal Walk, then journaling and a structured bedtime routine to manage anxiety and sleep.

professional.company: High-volume retail pharmacy inside a Kroger store in the Richmond area
projects: Training on Virginia’s PDMP workflow and controlled-substance documentation
Helping the lead tech clean up prior authorization queues and refill synchronization enrollments
Updating vaccine documentation processes for spring immunization demand
professional.performance_issues: He’s generally reliable but has had a few concentration lapses and increased sick days tied to insomnia/anxiety. He’s also sensitive to comments about weight/food in the break room, which can affect composure during busy shifts. He’s working with his manager to keep meal breaks consistent to support recovery.
workplace_relationships: Supportive pharmacist-in-charge, Dr. Elaine Carter, who schedules him with predictable breaks when possible
Occasional tension with a senior technician who jokes about dieting and ‘skipping lunch,’ which he finds triggering
Friendly rapport with front-end staff, but he keeps his personal life private

hobbies: Walking and light jogging on the Capital Trail
Cooking and meal prepping with new recipes (trying to make it enjoyable, not rule-based)
Reading sci-fi and memoirs from the Richmond Public Library
Casual photography around the James River parks
habits: Tracks sleep and anxiety symptoms in a private journal
Uses a structured meal plan and sets alarms to avoid skipping meals (a relapse-prevention strategy)
Late-night cleaning/rearranging the house when feeling restless after the kids’ departure
Limits alcohol and avoids caffeine after noon to reduce anxiety
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives alone in a two-bedroom apartment in Richmond that used to feel busy with the kids; he’s slowly converting the extra room into a small office/guest room but feels conflicted and lonely doing so.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Follows a recovery-oriented, balanced approach with regular meals and snacks; avoids rigid rules but tends to gravitate toward ‘safe’ foods when anxious. Tries to include protein and easy-to-digest options to manage reflux.
